item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I thought this was going to turn out to be some sort of training simulation that a GOI had been running in order to prepare an infiltration of the site in order to perform some sort of attack/interaction with E-8812 or whatever object/entity it was!

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed edit
- Hide

To connect this article to other content featuring the character of Director Gillespie the following edit is proposed:

A record of Director Shirley Gillespie's actions on January 11th, 1999 has been created.

to

A record of Director Shirley Gillespie's actions on January 11th, 1999 has been created.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Getting there! Not sure you caught everything, though. Still unanswered:

  • How did the 5/29 subject determine that the replica-people (who can't form intelligible sentences) were behaving passive-aggressively?
  • As noted previously, the cycle ends when the subject exits, so what does it mean to specify that the subject was ejected 'at the end of the cycle', as noted in the 6/14 test?
  • The 6/14 subject only spent 45 minutes inside the anomaly total. The notes say that they couldn't account for 'the additional time spent within SCP-2322.' Presumably that "additional" means "in addition to the time they spent in the simulation"… which means the research staff has concluded that it took significantly less than 45 minutes for the subject (an experienced Foundation contractor) to go from starting a fight to dead. I feel like that's a very meaningful fact about the simulation- that its replicas killed this person so fast- and yet it's barely stated here, and supported even less. There's no hint of how they drew that conclusion, and we know they have no account of what happened inside except what the subject could tell them from memory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made edits to address those concerns. Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three significant concerns, listed separately…

+ Replica personnel
- Replica personnel

In the main documentation, it says that the duplicate Site is a perfect replica, with replica objects and replica people &etc., and that the people either follow events perfectly or react to the subject's behavior if it's too far off- they'll escort you off site if you can't play the role well, they'll fight back if you start attacking them, they all act passive-aggressive to the one subject who didn't meet with E-8812.

But in the Essential Events Document, it repeatedly says that everyone in the replica Site is unintelligible when they talk, and all of Doctor Gillespie's paperwork is indecipherable.

This doesn't jibe with the experiment log, or even the rest of the description. If subjects can't understand anything the replica personnel say, how did the 5/29/02 subject know everyone was being passive-aggressive to them? How did the Foundation determine "All replicated personnel within SCP-2322 will identify the subject as Shirley Gillespie" if the replicated personnel can't say anything anyone will understand? More significantly, why is the unintelligibility not mentioned anywhere outside the events document?

I think the article would work fine with either perfect replica-people or unintelligible replica-people (maybe a subject figured out they were Dr. Gillespie by checking their reflection, and tested the theory by trying to use her security clearance), but it needs to stick to one or the other.

+ ## Gillespie
- ## Gillespie

Which is correct: "Director Gillespie," "Dr. Gillespie," or both? If both, I recommend changing the first reference to "… as Dr. Shirley Gillespie, who was Site-77's director at the time" and subsequently picking one title and using that consistently through the rest of the article.

+ Time in the simulation
- Time in the simulation

I'm not clear on how the loop effect works as seen from outside. I'm pretty sure that when someone goes in, they find themselves entering "Site-77" at the time and place Dr. Gillespie entered. I know that they stay inside until they leave (voluntarily or otherwise), at which point they find themselves in the scoring room and then get ejected. But does the simulation set their entrance time equivalent to Dr. Gillespie's arrival time and proceed from there, or does its clock match to the external clock? (Can you play Dr. Gillespie showing up late to work?) Does the length of time they spend inside the simulation take a matching length of time outside? What happens if you stay in the simulation after 11 PM internal time?

I would assume trivial answers to all of these questions (you go in and start from the beginning, elapsed time inside matches elapsed time outside, if you stay in too long the security or the simulation ejects you), if not for the experiment log with the subject who died in the simulation. The notes say the subject was ejected "45 minutes after ending" and "at the end of the cycle," which is confusing- does a normal run through the whole simulated day only take 45 minutes? If so, time is compressed inside, and that's an important property that isn't noted anywhere else. If not, what does "at the end of the cycle" mean, once it's decoupled from "when the subject finishes", and why doesn't the article answer that question anywhere?

I love this concept, especially including the total lack of obvious purpose or significance, but these things are really bugging me. No vote for now.

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: Behold: I Was Not Magnificent

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wonderfully bizarre. I love it.

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: So what would happen if Director Gillespie herself went through here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man there are so many fantastic time-themed scips in Series III. Loved this one as well.

Fantastic work, Roget. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, even if it kinda sorta reminds me of my own SCP-1746

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: Ok that is all my questions thank you for answering :D

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: Yes. 2322 treats all humans as Director Gillespie, so their anomalies are irrelevant because Director Gillespie wasn't anomalous.

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: Wait was the "Humanoid subject with anomalous properties" a SCP?

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: How about an SCP that is exactly humanoid in appearance? Like SCP 053?

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: It would be rejected by SCP-2322

item.title: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: Have you tried putting a non-humanoid sentient creature in SCP 2322? like SCP 682?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh ok

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not exactly, but it can appear that way at first glance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From what I understand this is like a game in which there is no permanent consequence and a score for a reward?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a thing that did some stuff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot! But there's something I don't end to understand… What does E-8812 is? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, this is weird. And I do love video game SCPs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said in chat: I love this. It's just so batshit weird, and not even in an over the top way. It's fascinating.

I also like the personality in the end note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Erm… LurkD, I think Lunamann was referring to the image in AndarielHalo's post.

It's not broken for me, though it is an animated thing, so that might be why it's not loading properly for them.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: Lunamann, articles are not required to have an image for your information. A broken image will be much more obvious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No it isn't

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Image is broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
will display a rank of the subject's performance on a scale of 1-10

This is when I started laughing. I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice work mr Roget

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This made me go "what the fuck" multiple times before I even finished the event log. One of my new fahvorites.

Also:

Procedure: Subject was an experienced Foundation contractor, instructed to obtain a weapon and begin attacking Site personnel indiscriminately, instead of going through the loop.

this is how it went in my head
– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enthusiastically upvoted. You know my opinion on this by now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yo dawg, I heard you like Site-77 so we put a Site-77 inside your Site-77.
But anyway, I really like do like this, I really do. Here, take its first upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for Silber thedeadlymoose and Cimmerian for the help.
